questions.question: What are political institutions?
questions.correct_answer: Organizations that help in making and implementing government decisions
questions.explanation: Political institutions ensure that governance follows rules and procedures in a democracy.

questions.question: Name the three major institutions in Indian democracy.
questions.explanation: These institutions together perform the functions of law making, policy implementation and dispute resolution.

questions.question: What is Parliament?
questions.correct_answer: The highest law-making body in the country
questions.explanation: Parliament represents the people and makes laws, controls finances and discusses national issues.

questions.question: What are the two houses of the Indian Parliament?
questions.explanation: Lok Sabha represents the people while Rajya Sabha represents the states.

questions.question: Who appoints the Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: The President appoints the leader of the majority party or coalition in the Lok Sabha as Prime Minister.

questions.question: What is the role of the Prime Minister?
questions.correct_answer: Head of the government and leader of the Council of Ministers
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister coordinates the work of different ministries and leads the government.

questions.question: What is the political executive?
questions.correct_answer: Elected leaders who take important policy decisions
questions.explanation: The political executive includes the Prime Minister and the Council of Ministers.

questions.question: Who are permanent executives?
questions.correct_answer: Civil servants who help implement government policies
questions.explanation: Permanent executives remain in office even when governments change.

questions.question: What is the judiciary?
questions.correct_answer: The system of courts that interprets laws and resolves disputes
questions.explanation: Judiciary protects the Constitution and ensures justice to citizens.

questions.question: What is judicial review?
questions.correct_answer: The power of courts to declare laws or actions unconstitutional
questions.explanation: Through judicial review, courts ensure that laws follow the Constitution.

notes.title: Role of Institutions in Democracy
notes.content: Institutions provide procedures and rules for decision making. They ensure that power is shared and decisions are taken carefully.

notes.title: Functions of Parliament
notes.content: Parliament makes laws, controls government spending, and discusses national policies and public issues.

notes.title: Executive Branch
notes.content: The executive implements laws and policies. It includes the President, Prime Minister and Council of Ministers.

notes.title: Judicial Independence
notes.content: The judiciary works independently from the legislature and executive to ensure fair judgments.

notes.title: Structure of Indian Judiciary
notes.content: The Indian judiciary includes the Supreme Court, High Courts and lower courts which together ensure justice.

facts.fact: The Lok Sabha has more power than the Rajya Sabha in financial matters.
The Prime Minister is the most powerful political institution in India.
Civil servants help ministers in implementing government policies.
The Supreme Court is the highest court in India.
